simplesect.para: These are only available when CONFIG_OSDP_MODE_PD is enabled.

briefdescription.para: Set callback method for PD command notification.

detaileddescription.para: This callback is invoked when the PD receives a command from the CP.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer that will be passed as the first argument of cb

briefdescription.para: API to notify PD events to CP.

detaileddescription.para: These events are sent to the CP as an alternate response to a POLL command.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to event struct. Must be filled by application.

simplesect.para: These are only available when CONFIG_OSDP_SC_ENABLED is enabled.

briefdescription.para: Get the current SC status mask.
Max length of text for text command.
Max length of key data for keyset command.
LED Colors as specified in OSDP for the on_color/off_color parameters.
Various card formats that a PD can support.

detaileddescription.para: This is sent to CP when a PD must report a card read.
After it has been registered with osdp_pd_set_command_callback, this method is invoked when the PD receives a command from the CP.

parameterdescription.para: pointer that will was passed to the arg param of osdp_pd_set_command_callback.
if LibOSDP must send a osdp_ACK response
if LibOSDP must send a osdp_NAK response
and modify the passed struct osdp_cmd *cmd if LibOSDP must send a specific response. This is useful for sending manufacturer specific reply osdp_MFGREP.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* cp_event_callback_t) (void *arg, int pd, struct osdp_event *ev)
memberdef.name: cp_event_callback_t

detaileddescription.para: After it has been registered with osdp_cp_set_event_callback, this method is invoked when the CP receives an event from the PD.

parameterdescription.para: Opaque pointer provided by the application during callback registration.
the offset (0-indexed) of this PD in kconfig OSDP_PD_ADDRESS_LIST
pointer to osdp_event struct (filled by libosdp).

briefdescription.para: Open Supervised Device Protocol (OSDP) public API header file.
